rationales.hook_strength: 'Why plain water isn't hydrating you' is specific and counter-intuitive enough to stop a scroll, though the sub-headline over-explains what should stay mysterious.
rationales.clarity: Each slide owns exactly one idea — myth, symptom, fix, food, balance — with a tight header and one actionable body line.
rationales.audience_fit: 'Afternoon brain fog' and 'chugging water and still tired' are precise pain points for the 20–35 busy-but-health-curious reader.
rationales.novelty: Electrolytes-not-just-water is a well-circulated angle (LMNT has mainstreamed it), so the framing is familiar even if the execution is clean.
rationales.brand_fit: Voice is plain and direct, health claims are hedged ('can all point to'), CTA ties naturally to the score mechanic — docks one point for 'actually counts' edging toward mild hype.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential because of the concrete pinch-of-salt tip, but novelty ceiling limits viral upside versus a truly surprising hook.
